purpose: The top-level application header bar that displays product branding, primary navigation, search, notifications, and user account. Fixed across all viewports.
whenToUse: As the topmost element of every SAP Fiori application
To provide consistent product identity and global navigation
When users need persistent access to search, notifications, and account
To house the hamburger menu trigger for the SideNavigation
whenNotToUse: For embedded UI inside another application
doRules: Place ShellBar as the first node in screen hierarchy
Use Branding Button slot for product name (max 2 lines)
Show only icons users need — hide others via boolean variants
Keep ShellBar height fixed at 52px regardless of variant
Use ShellSearch for global search; Shell icon buttons for global actions
dontRules: Do not place ShellBar inside another container with padding
Do not add custom buttons outside the supported slots
Do not change ShellBar background color — it is locked to sapShellColor
Do not duplicate ShellBar across nested views

layoutGuidance.placement: Topmost element, full viewport width, fixed position.
layoutGuidance.alignment: Branding left, navigation center, actions+avatar right.

contentGuidance.labelLength: Brand text ≤ 2 lines. Avatar shows initials or photo.
contentRules: Use the SAP logo only when authorized

responsiveBehavior.XL: Size=XL variant — full layout with all slots visible
responsiveBehavior.M: Size=M variant — middle slots may collapse to overflow
responsiveBehavior.S: Size=S variant — minimal: hamburger + brand + avatar; rest in overflow

accessibilityGuidance.requirement: Brand text contrast ≥ 4.5:1 against shell background
All shell icon buttons MUST have tooltips
Tab order: hamburger → brand → middle → right → avatar
Visible focus indicator on every interactive shell element
Notification badge state announced via aria-live

exceptions: Hamburger variant=False when SideNavigation is permanently visible
Joule (AI Copilot) button available only when AI features enabled
